channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The UN Is Looking at AI From the Wrong Side
item.description: The Geneva Digital Week opens this week with the inaugural Global Dialogue on AI Governance and the AI for Good Global Summit, where the United Nations’ new Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence will present to governments its first global scientific assessment of AI. The gathering caps three years in which the UN has ... Read more

item.title: From AI to ‘killer robots’: UN chief issues urgent governance call
item.description: UN chief António Guterres appealed on Monday for far-reaching, worldwide controls on Artificial Intelligence, as increasingly powerful AI chips that are designed for civilian use shift to the battlefield, where “killer robots” are already the norm.

item.title: UN maritime agency seeks release of 44 detained seafarers as food runs low
item.description: The UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO) appealed on Monday for the urgent release of 44 seafarers held captive in Somali waters by pirates and armed robbers.

item.title: Ukraine: Latest Russian assault leaves at least 14 dead in Kyiv
item.description: Russian strikes on the Ukrainian capital Kyiv killed at least 14 civilians and injured more than 80 others overnight on Monday, according to local authorities.

item.title: Sudan: Amid lethal drone attacks, children bear brunt of escalating war
item.description: Children across Sudan continue to bear the brunt of a war that is becoming increasingly deadly, with at least 330 children reported killed or injured during the first six months of 2026, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) said on Monday.

item.title: Venezuela quake: UN continues to scale up as damage estimate reaches $37 billion
item.description: The UN and its partners are ramping up assistance for people affected by the earthquakes in Venezuela, working alongside the Government's response effort, as a new UN estimate puts direct physical damage at $37 billion.

item.title: Afghanistan among the world’s largest displacement crises
item.description: Afghanistan faces one of the world’s biggest displacement crises amid poverty, drought and earthquakes, UN agencies warned on Monday.

item.title: Occupied Palestinian Territory: Aid restrictions in Gaza, ‘senseless’ infant deaths in the West Bank
item.description: Ongoing restrictions and closures of border crossings continue to hamper delivery of critical supplies into the Gaza Strip, amid mounting concern for children there and in the West Bank, the United Nations said on Monday.

item.title: CARICOM Leaders Gather in Saint Lucia as Caribbean Confronts Mounting Global, Regional Challenges
item.description: Caribbean leaders are meeting in Saint Lucia for their annual summit, confronting a convergence of global and regional challenges ranging from rising living costs and climate change to crime, food security and geopolitical tensions. The 51st Regular Meeting of the Conference of Heads of Government of the Caribbean Community (CARICOM), the regional organisation that promotes […]

item.title: Ebola Outbreak Could Cost Africa $3.6 Billion and Threaten Nearly One Million Livelihoods
item.description: A new assessment from the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) warns that the Ebola outbreak could cost Africa USD 3.6 billion, push 985,000 people into poverty, and put 300,000 jobs at risk. The new analysis shows that the damage extends well beyond just those infected, disproportionately harming vulnerable populations and creating trade disruptions, transport delays, […]

item.title: Africa’s Fourth Industrial Decade: From Political Mandate to Industrial Transformation
item.description: The UN has proclaimed 2026-2035 as the Fourth Industrial Development Decade for Africa (IDDA IV). What opportunities are there for Africa?
